metric_value: 25,000+ transactions per minute
notes: Industry-standard benchmark; exceeds Sun UltraEnterprise 6000 at 23,000 tpm with 16 CPUs

metric_value: Slightly over 23,000 tpm
notes: Comparison baseline; requires 16 CPUs vs AS/400e's 12

metric_value: 2,400 concurrent users
notes: Comparable to most powerful Unix server; almost double best-in-class NT server

metric_value: 4.6x (overall); 2x per-processor uniprocessor throughput
notes: IBM claims: about 2/3 from hardware, 1/3 from V4R1 firmware/software improvements

metric_name: High-end performance improvement projection for end-1998
metric_value: 70% further improvement over current 12-way
notes: IBM engineers in Rochester claim; Aberdeen reports as forward-looking statement

metric_value: 40% faster system rebuild; 60% faster IPL
notes: Addresses longtime AS/400 drawback; maintains 99.9%+ availability

metric_value: 20 GB main memory
notes: vs 2-4 GB limit on 32-bit Unix/NT; memory cost declining advantage

metric_name: AS/400 installed base at time of announcement
metric_value: 450,000 systems shipped to date
notes: 500,000 shipped total by end of 1997 per Wikipedia

metric_value: 85,000-100,000 System/36s still in production
notes: Year 2000 replacement opportunity for IBM

metric_value: ~250,000 proprietary midrange computers needing Y2K replacement
notes: Aberdeen assessment of broader Y2K replacement opportunity

metric_value: Targeting: e-business, Y2K replacement, fast-growing enterprises, data warehousing, central OLTP
notes: Five explicit new market positioning areas identified by Aberdeen

metric_value: AS/400e = integrated OS+DB+hardware; Unix/NT = component building blocks
notes: Integrated stack = faster deployment, simpler ops, lower TCO; Unix = more software choice; NT = 'good enough workgroup'

metric_value: Publicly declared intent to port all server-based applications to AS/400e
notes: Cited by Aberdeen as strong third-party validation; Netscape acquired by AOL in 1999

metric_value: Lotus Domino and Java scheduled for general availability Q1 1998 in native mode
notes: Object-based OS/400 architecture highly complementary to Java; IBM committing major development resources

metric_value: IBM has every possibility of re-igniting AS/400 revenue growth to over 20% per year for at least several years
notes: Conditional on IBM convincing business unit executives, not just IS

metric_value: Positioned in top 3-5 absolute commercial performance leaders for real-world production
notes: Aberdeen analysis based on CPW, TPC-C, SAP R/3 SD, RPMark95 benchmarks

metric_value: IBM rebranded AS/400 to eServer iSeries in 2000; continued to gain e-business workloads
notes: https://www.encora.com/interface/ibm-as400-historical-journey-and-future

metric_value: OS/400 renamed i5/OS (2004) then IBM i (2008); still in active development IBM i 7.5 (2022)
notes: https://power.fortra.com/blog/ibm-i-history-and-timeline

metric_name: Platform consolidated into IBM Power Systems
metric_value: System i and System p merged into IBM Power Systems in April 2008; IBM i continues as OS option
notes: Platform fully vindicated as enterprise-class; Aberdeen's 20%+ growth prediction approximately matched 1997-2002 period

metric_value: Sun acquired by Oracle for $7.4B in 2010; Solaris/SPARC platform declining
notes: Primary AS/400e competitor at time of study; ultimately less successful than IBM i platform

metric_value: Never having to say 'sorry, out of capacity' — 12-way SMP eliminates NT prototype failure mode
notes: One of five e-business positioning factors

metric_value: Never having to say 'ready in 6-12 months' — integrated stack enables fast Unix-competing deployment
notes: Two of five e-business positioning factors

metric_value: Ethical Hackers Association unable to penetrate AS/400e web security
notes: Three of five e-business positioning factors; notable third-party security validation

metric_value: Native Domino allows dynamic DB2/400 data pull into web-based e-business applications
notes: Four of five factors; Domino positioned as IBM's e-business application platform

metric_value: IBM porting each new Java release to AS/400e as soon as possible; object-based OS/400 highly complementary
notes: Five of five factors; strategic Java commitment in 1997 proved prescient

metric_value: 40%-45% price/performance improvement per year as AS/400 division goal
notes: Aberdeen assessment of IBM's stated objective
